title: Radiogrammitis coredrosora
taxonomy: Radiogrammitis coredrosora is a species within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly referred to as the polypody family. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The division Pteridophyta is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, emphasizing its status as a vascular plant. As a member of the family Polypodiaceae, Radiogrammitis coredrosora is a type of epiphytic fern. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Radiogrammitis coredrosora, also known as the Pacific Grammitid fern or Pacific Rockcap, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of the Pacific region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, as well as potential treatments for fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Radiogrammitis coredrosora include various polyphenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Radiogrammitis coredrosora is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and may also be used in the form of a tincture or decoction to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
